name: Signal
tagline: Collect and preserve the raw observation layer.
why: AI Radar starts before synthesis: external feeds, official updates, agent repos, friction signals, subscriptions, and manual uploads all need to become durable signal records before they can support judgment.
how: Run scheduled collectors for RSS, official sources, GitHub, Hacker News, Product Hunt, and manual-promoted sessions
Normalize source metadata, topic, provenance, score, subscription priority, and processing tier
Persist latest and historical signal outputs with collector and pipeline metrics for operational visibility

name: Insight
why: A fluent summary is not enough. Current development centers on evidence packs, claim extraction, claim verification, VerifiedInsight metadata, and action gates so weak evidence does not become overconfident downstream work.
how: Generate signal insight with model routing, execution policy, fallback policy, and output validation
Attach evidence sufficiency, low-evidence gates, provenance, reason codes, claim support, and verification status
Expose verified insight and blocked or allowed downstream actions in Signal Detail and Project Takeaway candidate creation

name: Reflection
tagline: Keep human cognition separate from factual evidence.
why: Reflection is long-horizon context, not proof for external claims. AI Radar uses manual notes, saved sessions, reflection views, and career context to preserve judgment without letting private cognition masquerade as evidence.
how: Save manual analysis sessions with upload intent, review priority, cognitive layer, and generated insight context
Link manual-upload-derived signals back to their source session and show generation and provenance context
Keep deep reflection content flexible and human-authored; derive structured judgment only when review moments are explicit

name: Knowledge Engine
tagline: Synthesize signals across topics, time, and product surfaces.
why: The system should not collapse into a feed of isolated items. The knowledge layer turns signal and insight history into radar summaries, topic movement, Agent Watch, Friction Signals, and project-relevant intelligence.
how: Compute topic classification, trend, momentum, rising-topic, weekly summary, and strategic-priority outputs
Build specialized intelligence surfaces such as Radar, Agent Watch, Friction Signals, and career-relevant views
Use project relevance mapping to connect structured intelligence with active work without treating similarity as final judgment

name: Project Engine
tagline: Convert verified intelligence into reviewable work.
why: The current product direction moved away from Decision Cards as the main object. Project Takeaways, Watch, Action, Review Records, Trajectory, and Calibration now carry the decision loop.
how: Route verified or manually overridden signals into Project Takeaway candidates with human review gates
Support confirm, reject, dismiss, watch, action, action completion, and workspace completion flows
Persist Review Records and Calibration Events so the system can learn from outcome quality instead of only creating tasks
